query:
INSERT INTO drillDetails (drillId, skillSet, difficulty, athletes, time, equipment, goal, description, instructions, createdBy, createdAt, updatedAt)
VALUES (
  '3-plate-adjustment-drill',
  'Hitting',
  'Medium',
  '1+',
  '15 minutes',
  '3 home plates, L-screen, baseballs, bat',
  'Develop a hitter''s timing and adjustability to different pitch speeds without changing their core swing mechanics.',
  '["Set up three home plates in a line toward the pitcher, spaced approximately 3–5 feet apart (typically at 10, 15, and 20 feet from the tosser).", "A coach stands behind an L-screen and delivers front-toss pitches at a constant speed while the batter takes 3–5 swings at each plate, starting from the back and progressing forward (or vice versa).", "Because the toss speed never changes, the distance determines the difficulty: the back plate simulates a slow off-speed pitch requiring the hitter to stay back and wait, while the front plate simulates a high-velocity fastball that forces the hitter to load earlier and use quicker hands.", "The goal is for the hitter to maintain their core swing mechanics while adjusting their timing and rhythm so the front foot is down before contact at every station."]',
  'Set up three home plates in a line toward the pitcher, spaced approximately 3–5 feet apart (typically at 10, 15, and 20 feet from the tosser). A coach stands behind an L-screen and delivers front-toss pitches at a constant speed while the batter takes 3–5 swings at each plate, starting from the back and progressing forward (or vice versa). Because the toss speed never changes, the distance determines the difficulty: the back plate simulates a slow off-speed pitch requiring the hitter to stay back and wait, while the front plate simulates a high-velocity fastball that forces the hitter to load earlier and use quicker hands. The goal is for the hitter to maintain their core swing mechanics while adjusting their timing and rhythm so the front foot is down before contact at every station.',
  1,
  NOW(),
  NOW()
);

  'Set up three home plates in a line toward the pitcher, spaced approximately 3–5 feet apart (typically at 10, 15, and 20 feet from the tosser). A coach stands behind an L-screen and delivers front-toss pitches at a constant speed while the batter takes 3–5 swings at each plate, starting from the back and progressing forward (or vice versa). Because the toss speed never changes, the distance determines the difficulty: the back plate simulates a slow off-speed pitch requiring the hitter to stay back and wait, while the front plate simulates a high-velocity fastball that forces the hitter to load earlier and use quicker hands.
